title: House Robber II
problem_statement: Extend House Robber to a circular street where the first and last houses are adjacent.
intuition: Solve two linear House Robber variations excluding either the first or last house.

approaches.name: Two-pass DP for circular array
approaches.description: Compute the best robbery excluding the first house and excluding the last house, then take the maximum.
approaches.why_not: Trying all circular combinations would be inefficient; reduce to linear cases instead.

interview_qa.q: Why split the circular problem into two ranges?
interview_qa.a: Because either the first or last house must be excluded to avoid the circular adjacency conflict.

interview_qa.q: What if nums has only one house?
interview_qa.a: Return its value directly because there is no adjacency restriction beyond itself.
